title: What the jury will actually decide in the case of Elon Musk vs. Sam Altman
description: Here's what the biggest tech court case of the year is all about.

title: Elon Musk’s SpaceXAI has been bleeding staff since its merger
description: More than 50 employees have reportedly left Elon Musk’s newly merged SpaceXAI since February, raising questions about burnout, leadership changes, talent poaching, and whether liquidity events weakened retention incentives.

title: OpenAI says Codex is coming to your phone
description: The update gives users enhanced flexibility over how they can manage their workflows.

title: What happens when AI starts building itself?
description: Richard Socher's new $650 million startup wants to build an AI that can research and improve itself indefinitely — and he insists it will actually ship products.

title: OpenAI is reportedly preparing legal action against Apple; it wouldn’t be the first partner to feel burned
description: OpenAI is so frustrated with Apple over a ChatGPT integration that failed to deliver the subscribers and prominence it expected that the company is now actively exploring legal action against the iPhone maker.

title: Clawdmeter turns your Claude Code usage stats into a tiny desktop dashboard
description: A new open source gadget called Clawdmeter turns Claude Code usage stats into a tiny desktop dashboard for AI coding power users.

title: Cerebras raises $5.5B, then stock pops $108%, in the first huge tech IPO of 2026
description: A year ago, it looked like this day would never happen for Cerebras.

title: Khosla Ventures is betting $10M on Ian Crosby, whose first startup, Bench, imploded
description: Crosby is building Synthetic, a fully autonomous AI bookkeeping service for other startups.

title: Cisco cuts nearly 4,000 jobs to spend more on AI, reports ‘record quarterly revenue’
description: This is Cisco's latest layoff in recent years, while the company's chief executive touts record revenue and growth.

title: Two weeks left: Startup Battlefield 200 applications close May 27
description: Your shot at VC access, global visibility, TechCrunch coverage, and $100K equity-free funding is running out. Deadline to apply is May 27. Apply now.

title: Wirestock raises $23M to supply creative multimodal data to AI labs
description: The company pivoted to being a data provider in 2023 and now supplies datasets of images, videos, design assets, and gaming and 3D content to AI labs.

title: Who decides what AI tells you? Campbell Brown, once Meta’s news chief, has thoughts
description: "The conversation is sort of happening in Silicon Valley around one thing, and a totally different conversation is happening among consumers," Campbell Brown said at StrictlyVC.

title: Clio’s $500M milestone arrives just as Anthropic ups the ante
description: Legal tech startups, including Clio, which just hit $500 million in ARR, are seeing massive customer adoption.

title: Notion just turned its workspace into a hub for AI agents
description: Notion’s new developer platform lets teams connect AI agents, external data sources, and custom code directly into their workspace as the company pushes deeper into agentic productivity software.

title: Musk’s xAI is running nearly 50 gas turbines unchecked at its Mississippi data center
description: Gas turbines at xAI's Colossus 2 data center have drawn a lawsuit over the company's use of "mobile" gas turbines as power plants.

title: Anthropic’s Cat Wu says that, in the future, AI will anticipate your needs before you know what they are
description: The head of product for Claude Code and Cowork says that the next big step for AI is proactivity.

title: Who trusts Sam Altman?
description: "I believe I am an honest and trustworthy businessperson," Altman testified in federal court.

title: Origin Lab raises $8M to help video game companies sell data to world-model builders
description: Origin Lab will serve as a marketplace where AI labs can buy high-quality licensed data, and video-game companies can sell it.

title: Anthropic courts a new kind of customer: small business owners
description: For founders and investors, Anthropic's new offering signals that the AI platform wars are expanding downmarket and that the next major battleground for user acquisition isn't the Fortune 500; it's the 36 million small businesses that make up the backbone of the U.S. economy.

title: Amazon launches an AI shopping assistant for the search bar, powered by Alexa+
description: Alexa for Shopping offers a voice- and touch-enabled shopping experience across mobile, desktop, and Echo Show smart displays. Alexa for Shopping provides more personalized recommendations and automates the shopping experience across Amazon and other online retailers.
